statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: Archived audit trail only. Keep this out of the normal tweak surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: KeQueryDpcWatchdogInformation function
links.summary: Microsoft documents that Windows implements a DPC watchdog timer and describes how the system detects prolonged DPC execution and can bug check with DPC_WATCHDOG_VIOLATION (0x133). The page confirms the feature area but does not publish the DpcWatchdogPeriod registry key.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Avoiding DPC Watchdog timeout problems in StorPort Miniports
links.summary: Microsoft documents the DPC watchdog feature from the driver-behavior side and explains that Windows can decide a prolonged high-IRQL routine has taken excessive time to finish. The page confirms the watchdog feature area but does not publish the registry key.

links.title: Decompiled DPC watchdog configuration reader
links.summary: The decompiled watchdog configuration reader checks KeDpcWatchdogPeriodMs and copies it into the result block when present, proving the raw registry-backed period is consumed by kernel code.

links.title: Repo system research notes for kernel registry values
links.summary: The repo documents DpcWatchdogPeriod = 120000 as personal research and ties it to KeDpcWatchdogPeriodMs.

links.title: Current app implementation
links.summary: The provider writes DpcWatchdogPeriod = 120000 for the current tweak.

links.title: Decompiled DPC watchdog configuration reader
links.summary: The decompiled watchdog configuration reader checks KeDpcWatchdogPeriodMs and copies it into the result block when present, proving the raw watchdog period value is consumed by kernel code.
